meta.title: API & SDK Documentation
meta.description: Integration guide for B2B Partners to integrate NYX's secure messaging engine.

hero.title: API & SDK Integration
hero.subtitle: Comprehensive guide for enterprise partners to embed NYX's Zero-Knowledge messaging engine directly into your applications.

request.title: 1. Request Access
request.p1: To utilize the NYX Engine APIs or SDKs, developers must first obtain an x-nyx-engine-key. We currently enforce a manual vetting process for all third-party integrations.
request.footer: Include your target use case, expected daily active users (DAU), and whether you require an on-premise dedicated relay or will use the shared cloud relay.

iframe.title: 2. Method A: Iframe Integration
iframe.p1: The fastest way to embed NYX is via an isolated Iframe. First, create a secure room URL by hitting our S2S API: POST /api/engine/rooms. Then, embed the response URL in your frontend.

sdk.title: 3. Method B: Native SDK
sdk.p1: For deep custom UI control, install the local SDK. This grants you raw access to the messaging bus and local cryptography worker without using our UI.

errors.base64_sol: Ensure you are using URL-Safe Base64 with NO padding before feeding data to crypto_box_seal_open.
errors.csp_sol: You must provide your domains to our team to whitelist them in the Content-Security-Policy headers of the relay server.
errors.memory_sol: If using raw Libsodium, ensure you explicitly call sodium.memzero(key) on symmetric keys after encryption/decryption cycles to free memory.
errors.cors_sol: Media uploads must hit the R2 proxy endpoint /api/engine/upload, not the bucket directly, to resolve strict CORS preflight checks.
